lectures.title: Motion in One Dimension
lectures.unit_summary: Build the language of motion first, then connect it to graphs and acceleration models before tackling harder mechanics.
lectures.summary: Start with position, displacement, and average velocity so later graph and acceleration work has a clean conceptual base.

lectures.title: Velocity-Time Graphs
lectures.unit_summary: Build the language of motion first, then connect it to graphs and acceleration models before tackling harder mechanics.
lectures.summary: Translate motion descriptions into velocity-time graphs and use slope or area cues without guessing from the picture alone.

lectures.title: Constant Acceleration Models
lectures.unit_summary: Build the language of motion first, then connect it to graphs and acceleration models before tackling harder mechanics.
lectures.summary: Use constant-acceleration relationships to connect changing velocity, elapsed time, and displacement in one coherent model.
